page: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM, configured with a 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. Powered by an x86_64 Intel processor, the instance allocates 1 physical core with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. The processor includes a cache hierarchy consisting of 48 KB L1d, 2 MB L2, and 260 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated vector and matrix operations, alongside hardware-assisted cryptography via AES and SHA_NI. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the n4-standard-2 delivers moderate single-core capabilities but exhibits limitations under multi-threaded and memory-intensive workloads. Single-core performance is rated as average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1684.29 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0. However, multi-core and memory subsystem performance are comparatively weak. The stress-ng multi-core score is 1714.68, and the Passmark memory score is 838.1, reflecting poor memory bandwidth, which drops to 19.35 GB/sec for large-block read operations. Software compilation and ray tracing benchmarks also fall into the lower performance tiers.

Given its hardware profile and benchmark results, the n4-standard-2 is positioned as a cost-efficient entry-level option for workloads that do not require high memory throughput or intensive multi-threaded processing. It is suited for basic web serving, light text processing, and simple single-threaded applications. It can handle lightweight database operations and small-scale LLM inference, achieving 191.93 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 135M parameter model, though it is not designed for demanding enterprise databases, heavy data science jobs, or complex parallel computing.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance configured with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel processor with 1 physical core and 2 threads, it features a dedicated CPU allocation and a 260 MB L3 cache. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel AMX, but lacks bundled local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data shows average single-core performance, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1834.0, but weak multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. This instance is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and small-scale text processing tasks where resource density and qualitative cost efficiency are prioritized over high-throughput parallel computing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is an x86_64 general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and an Intel processor. It offers average single-core performance for lightweight workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n4-standard-2 is a general-purpose instance with 2 vCPUs and 8 GB RAM, suitable for basic single-threaded workloads.
tagline: An entry-level general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel cores and advanced instruction sets for lightweight single-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: Features 2 vCPUs and 8 GB of RAM with a dedicated x86_64 Intel processor.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for vector and matrix operations.
Delivers average single-core performance but exhibits weak multi-core and memory bandwidth.
No bundled local storage, GPU accelerators, or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.
Best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic single-threaded tasks.
